name: Trino Python Client (Distributed SQL Query Engine)

auth.notes: HTTP basic auth, JWT, Kerberos, OAuth2 (OIDC), certificate auth

performance.notes: Network to Trino coordinator; query latency depends on cluster size and data

agent_readiness.notes: Python DB-API 2.0 client for Trino distributed SQL. import trino. conn = trino.dbapi.connect(host='trino.co', port=8080, user='user', catalog='hive', schema='default'). cursor = conn.cursor(). cursor.execute('SELECT * FROM table LIMIT 10'). rows = cursor.fetchall(). SQLAlchemy dialect: trino+trino://. pandas: pd.read_sql(query, engine). Supports Hive, Iceberg, Delta, Glue catalogs. Federated queries across S3/GCS/HDFS. Query cancellation.

gotchas: Requires running Trino cluster (not embedded like DuckDB)
Large query result streaming can be slow over HTTP
Kerberos auth setup complex in enterprise environments
Trino coordinator is single point of failure (HA requires coordination)
best_when: Federated SQL queries across multiple data sources (S3, HDFS, RDBMS)
Querying Hive/Iceberg/Delta Lake tables at petabyte scale
Data lake SQL analytics with pandas integration
Interactive analytics on AWS Glue catalog data
avoid_when: Small datasets (DuckDB much simpler, no cluster needed)
summary: Trino Python client provides DB-API 2.0 and SQLAlchemy access to Trino's distributed SQL engine. trino.dbapi.connect(host, port, catalog='hive') then cursor.execute(SQL). Federated queries across Hive/Iceberg/Delta Lake/S3/Glue. SQLAlchemy dialect for pandas/ORM integration. Petabyte-scale analytics. Requires Trino cluster — use DuckDB for local analytics without cluster overhead.
